title: Dungeon Defenders: Awakened
description: Gameplay and systems programming at Chromatic Games: built the passive-ability system powering hundreds of abilities, the cross-platform DLC unlocking system, and an in-game store. Sole programmer on the PS4/PS5 port and rewrote the multiplayer session code across all platforms.
highlights: Created the underlying passive-ability system that now supports hundreds of individual abilities.
Built the cross-platform DLC unlocking system across Steam, Xbox, PlayStation, and Switch.
Sole programmer on the PS4/PS5 port of the game.
Rewrote the multiplayer session code for all platforms after PlayFab 1.0 was deprecated.
Created an in-game store using PlayFab Economy v2 and Steam Inventory.
Oversaw the port to the Stove platform (multiplayer via Epic Online Services / EOS Redpoint) and a physical-media Switch release with Super Rare.

title: Dungeon Defenders: Going Rogue
description: Feature and systems work at Chromatic Games on the studio's roguelike entry in the Dungeon Defenders franchise, including UI, abilities, characters, and boss AI.
highlights: Added UI, AI, ability, and character features throughout the game.
Extended the shared passive-ability system to support Going Rogue's roguelike progression.
Worked on the boss AI for the Lycan King boss fight.

title: Dungeon Defenders 2 — Backend
description: Backend and live-service work on Dungeon Defenders 2, spearheading upgrades to the game's backend and building a serverless Twitch Drops integration.
highlights: Spearheaded upgrades to the live Dungeon Defenders 2 backend (C#/AWS/MySQL).
Built a serverless Twitch Drops system using AWS Lambda, DynamoDB, and API Gateway.

title: Primiri Training Tool
description: Lead programmer on a training/simulation tool at RhinoCorps, creating the majority of the code and user interface along with core Unreal Engine tooling.
highlights: Lead programmer on the Primiri training tool — created a majority of the code and user interface.
Built a RuntimeImporter plugin in Unreal 4 (FBX SDK + Assimp) for loading 3D models at runtime.
Drove AutoCAD-to-3D pipeline improvements that cut VRAM usage by 40%.
Prototyped a networked first-person shooter that could connect to a real-world combat simulation.
